questions.prompt: Why is it challenging to achieve consensus across nodes?
What do we want to ensure in a distributed system?
Can multiple servers improve the accuracy of time synchronization?
options: C) It depends on network delays

questions.prompt: What can happen with variable network delays in a distributed system?
options: A) Nonlinearizable behavior will not occur
B) Linearizable behavior will always occur

questions.prompt: Why is fault detection and consensus important?
options: A) Because it's fun to do
C) For the sake of network delays
C) It depends on the type of failure

questions.prompt: What do we need to ensure in a distributed system?
options: C) Fault tolerance is not necessary

questions.prompt: If a node is accidentally firewalled off from NTP servers, what may happen to its synchronization accuracy?
options: A) A minimum error of 35 ms is achievable
B) The network delay becomes more accurate
C) The NTP client gives up entirely

questions.prompt: What happens if a transaction has been committed on one node, but not on others?
options: A) The nodes become inconsistent with each other
B) The transaction is automatically retried
C) The commit record is rolled back
